title: Piquant Algerian Barley Tagine
description: A silky, malty tagine with a tangy vinegar base, finished flambé on a cedar plank for an adventurous Algerian flare.

ingredients.name: olive-oil
ingredients.notes: add salt and pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C and place a lightly oiled cedar‑plank on a baking sheet, letting it sit.
In a pot, bring 3 cups water to a boil, add 1 cup pearl barley, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until barley is tender; drain excess liquid.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat, add 1 cup tomato puree, 1 cup diced tomatoes, 3 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, and 1/2 tsp smoked paprika, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Pour the sauce over the cooked barley on the cedar‑plank, spreading evenly.
Sprinkle salt and pepper to taste over the mixture.
Cover the cedar‑plank with foil and bake for 25 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove foil, then flambé the top by igniting 1 tsp clarified butter, letting the flames subside for a caramelized crust.
Let the tagine rest for 5 minutes, then garnish with chopped fresh coriander before serving.
